snp1.5:1.0.83: “I ask the muni, one of abundant wisdom,”“the Buddha, lord of the Dhamma, rid of craving,supreme among bipeds, the most excellent of trainers:how many ascetics are there in the world? Please tell me this.” (1)
snp1.5:1.0.84: “There are four kinds of ascetics; there is no fifth,“Being asked in person, let me explain them to you:the conqueror of the path,the teacher of the path,the one who lives on the path, and the defiler of the path.” (2)
snp1.5:1.0.85: “Whom do the buddhas call a conqueror of the path?”“How is one without equal as a shower of the path?Being asked, tell me about one who lives on the path,and explain to me the defiler of the path.” (3)
snp1.5:1.0.87: “One here who, having known the supreme as supreme,explains and analyzes the Dhamma right here,the muni who has cut off doubt, without impulse,is the second bhikkhu,whom they call a teacher of the path. (5)
snp1.5:1.0.88: “The one on the well-taught trail of the Dhammawho lives on the path, controlled and mindful,resorting to blameless ways of conduct,is the third bhikkhu, whom they call one living on the path. (6)
snp1.5:1.0.89: “Having taken on the dress of the disciplined,one who is brazen, a corrupter of families, impudent,hypocritical, uncontrolled, chaff,living as a dissembler: he is the defiler of the path. (7)
snp1.5:1.0.90: “When a householder who is learned, wise,a noble disciple, has penetrated these, [18]since he knows, ‘They are not all like him,’on seeing such, he does not forsake his faith.For how could one equate them:the undefiled with the defiled, the pure with the impure?” (8)
